title: Frontend Authentication Security Audit
scope: planner-frontend, preference-frontend, workforce-frontend — auth callback, middleware, AuthProvider, layout, dashboard-header

findings.description: Open redirect vulnerability: the `next` query parameter is used unvalidated in NextResponse.redirect(), allowing an attacker to craft a link with ?next=https://evil.com to redirect victims post-authentication.

tasks.description: Validate `next` parameter in planner-frontend callback: reject absolute URLs and protocol-relative paths; fall back to '/'.

findings.description: Silent production failure: SUPABASE_ANON_KEY falls back to an empty string when VSO injection fails, causing every Supabase request to return 401 with no startup error. The pod appears healthy but is fully broken.

tasks.description: Add production guard in planner-frontend config.ts: throw an error at module load time if SUPABASE_ANON_KEY is empty and NODE_ENV is production.
Add production guard in preference-frontend config.ts.
Add production guard in workforce-frontend config.ts.

findings.description: Token issuer not verified: the session returned by the Edge Function fetch is passed to setSession() without checking the JWT `iss` claim. A misconfigured or compromised Edge Function could supply a token from a different Supabase project.

tasks.description: In planner-frontend callback, decode the JWT payload and verify iss starts with SUPABASE_URL before calling setSession. Redirect to auth-code-error on mismatch.
Same iss claim check in preference-frontend callback.
Same iss claim check in workforce-frontend callback.

findings.description: preference-frontend AuthProvider uses supabaseRef.current inside signOut and refreshClaims closures. planner-frontend uses useMemo for a stable client — the same pattern should be applied to preference to eliminate stale-ref risk and ensure consistency.

tasks.description: Replace supabaseRef pattern in preference AuthProvider with useMemo client. Update signOut and refreshClaims to use the memoized reference.

findings.description: Middleware login redirect constructs the callback URL without a `next` parameter. After authentication, users always land at `/` rather than their intended destination, breaking deep links and degrading UX.

tasks.description: In planner-frontend middleware, append next=encodeURIComponent(pathname+search) to the callbackUrl before forwarding to the Edge Function.

findings.description: layout.tsx calls getSession() server-side but discards the result. AuthProvider receives only initialUser (no initialSession), causing a client-side waterfall re-fetch and a flash of unauthenticated content before hydration.

tasks.description: Capture the getSession() result in planner layout.tsx and pass it as initialSession to AuthProvider.

findings.description: createClient() is called inside a useEffect body that depends on the user object, causing a new Supabase SDK instance to be constructed on every auth state event (including token refreshes).

tasks.description: Memoize the Supabase client in dashboard-header.tsx with useMemo([], []). Change effect dep from user to user?.id to avoid re-fetching on token refresh.

clarifications.decision: The iss claim check is a misconfiguration guard, not a cryptographic signature verification. Signature validation is handled by Supabase server-side. This is the appropriate trust boundary given the Edge Function is first-party infrastructure.
The Edge Function is confirmed to pass the redirect_to parameter intact, so encoding next into the callbackUrl before forwarding will survive the round-trip.

This audit is registered retrospectively. All findings were identified and implemented in conversation 0e378e24-33e7-4919-969e-88478c6b34c0 before the audit files were created. The feature branch (audit/260321-frontend-auth-security) was created on the same day as implementation. Implementation branches in the three target repos are named audit/260319-cicd-workflow-health (the concurrent CI/CD audit branch; work was committed there before this audit's branch was formally created).

implementationPhases.name: Critical Security Fixes
implementationPhases.rationale: Must Fix: open redirect, silent credential failure, and unverified session token are production security risks requiring immediate remediation.

implementationPhases.name: High Severity and UX
implementationPhases.rationale: Deep-link preservation is high severity (broken UX + potential for auth bypass edge cases) and shares the same callback/middleware surface as phase 1.

implementationPhases.name: Consistency and Performance
implementationPhases.rationale: Should Fix: architectural drift and client-side waterfall are quality improvements that reduce future risk but have no immediate security impact.
